basics.name: Jonathan King
basics.label: Lead AI/ML Engineer | DevOps/SRE | MLOps
basics.summary: Engineering leader spanning AI/ML systems, MLOps, and cloud infrastructure. Experience includes building production ML services, compliant platforms, and multi-tenant AWS/GCP environments across SaaS, healthcare, retail, hospitality, and enterprise workloads.

work.summary: Led AI/ML engineering for central AI/ML engineering excellence team across 92 portfolio companies.
highlights: Led 6 engineers while owning modeling, architecture, and quality decisions across hospitality, retail, healthcare, and automotive workloads.

Shipped 6 production AI/ML systems in 6 months, including churn prediction via survival analysis serving millions of customers and a recommendation system using hybrid embedding retrieval with a custom reranker.
Designed and prototyped agentic generation of knowledge graphs with embedded node semantics, enabling natural-language queries across several databases without a unified schema.
Architected the ecommerce business unit's EKS platform with ephemeral per-PR environments in GitHub Actions, cutting PR merge time from days to hours.
Built an MLOps stack on SageMaker, MLflow, and Prometheus, reducing the full train-to-serve pipeline from days to hours.

work.summary: Owned infrastructure during the company's transition from NLP analytics to LLM-based workloads.
highlights: Implemented per-environment AWS account isolation, network redundancy, and full infrastructure-as-code coverage, cutting provisioning from days to minutes.
Architected infrastructure with Terraform and Terragrunt across dozens of tenant-specific environments, spanning AWS-native deployments and hybrid enterprise customer environments.
Owned the SOC 2 Type II program end to end, including identity, infrastructure controls, patching, and audit preparation as the sole internal security function.

work.summary: Managed GCP infrastructure for a real-time clinical decision support platform and clinical analysis.
highlights: Supported KATE, an FDA Breakthrough Device-designated AI platform processing 10B+ data points across 4M+ emergency department patient encounters.
Owned infrastructure for the Clinical Data Engine, enabling research-grade retrospective analysis and real-time clinical NLP across millions of EHR records.
Improved analytics application response times from 2 minutes to under 10 seconds through BigQuery view optimization.

work.summary: First full infrastructure hire for a real-time patient monitoring platform.
highlights: Architected the AWS cloud platform with SOC 2 and HIPAA compliance for real-time patient monitoring.
Built the end-to-end IoT-to-cloud ML inference pipeline from Bluetooth patient devices through edge gateways and custom DNS routing into a centralized Kubernetes cluster.
Supported ML-serving workloads including posture recognition, tachycardia prediction, and sepsis detection.
Developed and maintained GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines for major services to production, pairing with lead developers on releases.

work.summary: Kubernetes SME for a large-scale migration from legacy VM environments to AKS.
highlights: Led containerization guidance for workloads serving billions in transaction volume during the migration from VMs and Rackspace to AKS.
Designed and delivered Kubernetes training for 25+ engineers across US and offshore teams.
Built the Prometheus/Grafana observability stack for Kubernetes environments, including dashboards, alerting rules, and runbooks.

publications.name: Decoding Odor Mixtures in the Dog Brain: An Awake fMRI Study
Through a Dog's Eyes: fMRI Decoding of Naturalistic Videos from the Dog Cortex
